message: agent2 here: implementing StreamListener debug/trace logging feature in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py (adding debug flag, guarded DEBUG logs at start/end/emit/flush/reset). Will only touch this file. Let me know if you're also editing it.

agent1 here: implementing idle_timeout_s / tick() heartbeat feature in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. Adding: __init__ param idle_timeout_s + self._last_chunk_ts; receive() gets a small addition near the top to refresh _last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunk_message; new tick(now_ts=None) method appended after flush(). Should be additive/non-conflicting with your debug logging, but shout if your reset refactor touches the same reset-state lines (stream_start/stream_end/field_start_queue/field_end_queue/cache_hit) in receive().

Ack — I did touch the reset-state block in receive() (the 'if self.stream_end: if self.allow_reuse:' branch) to add a single debug log line after 'self.stream_start = False': `if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG): self._logger.debug(f"State reset for field '{self.signature_field_name}' (allow_reuse=True)")`. That's the only change in that block — I didn't touch cache_hit/field_start_queue/field_end_queue lines themselves, just appended after. Should merge fine with your tick()/_last_chunk_ts additions as long as you don't reorder that block. I also added a module-level 'import logging' at the top and a DEBUG_PREVIEW_LENGTH constant + _truncate_preview() helper function right after the ADAPTER_SUPPORT_STREAMING list. Also added debug logs in flush() (restructured the return statements into a truncated_buffer variable, then logs, then returns it at the end) and in the start-detection/rolling-end-check/emit-chunk spots inside receive(). Let me know if your tick() also touches flush() or the end-of-stream detection blocks.

Thanks — my changes are now on disk in streaming_listener.py: (1) 'import time' added after 'import re'; (2) __init__ signature adds idle_timeout_s param + docstring line + self.idle_timeout_s/self._last_chunk_ts assignments right after self.allow_reuse; (3) in receive(), right after the try/except that extracts chunk_message (before the 'if chunk_message and start_identifier in chunk_message' line), I added a 2-line block: 'if chunk_message: self._last_chunk_ts = time.time()'; (4) new tick(now_ts=None) method appended right after flush() ends (before find_predictor_for_stream_listeners). I don't touch flush()'s internals or the end-of-stream detection blocks inside receive() — only that one small ts-refresh insertion near the top of receive(). Should be safe to layer your debug logging on top; just re-read the file before editing since it currently only has my changes applied.
